questions.question: What percentage of sales rep underperformance is due to management gaps, not talent gaps?
questions.explanation: 70% of sales rep underperformance is due to management gaps. Before making a fire decision, audit your own management quality. The question isn't always 'is this the right person?' — it's also 'am I managing them the right way?'

questions.question: What is the recommended management cadence for the first 90 days?

options.text: Weekly 30-minute 1:1 plus 2 call reviews per week
No scheduled meetings — check in when needed

questions.explanation: Weekly 30-minute 1:1, 2 call reviews per week, and a specific coaching focus each week. Not general 'be better' feedback — one specific skill per week. The first 90 days of management determine the next 9 months.

questions.question: Which of these is the 'Absentee Manager' failure mode?

options.text: Reviewing every email before it sends
No weekly 1:1s, no call reviews, just checking meeting count at month end

questions.explanation: The Absentee Manager has no 1:1s, no call reviews, no specific feedback. They check the meeting count at month end and wonder why it's low. Without frequent coaching, bad habits solidify in the first 90 days.

questions.question: Hiring a sales rep frees up your time immediately from Day 1.
questions.explanation: False. Month 1: 5-7 hours/week on onboarding. Month 2: 3-4 hours on supervision and coaching. Month 3+: 1-2 hours if going well. Management time decreases sharply as the hire ramps, but the early investment is necessary.

questions.question: Which management failure mode are you most likely to fall into: Absentee Manager or Micromanager? What specific management cadence would you establish for your first hire's first 90 days?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response honestly identifies their likely failure mode, designs a specific management cadence (weekly 1:1 time, call review frequency, coaching focus areas), and demonstrates understanding that early management investment prevents later performance problems.
